case_a.text: Elena volunteers to read Scripture aloud during the service because she knows several older members can follow along more easily when the reading is slow and clear.
case_a.outward_act_summary: Volunteers to read Scripture aloud during the service.
case_a.motive_summary: Serve the congregation, especially older members.
case_a.consequence_summary: The reading is delivered slowly and clearly for the congregation.
case_a.rule_summary: Offer a public service task faithfully.

case_b.text: Elena volunteers to read Scripture aloud during the service because she wants people to talk afterward about how spiritually impressive she sounded.
case_b.outward_act_summary: Volunteers to read Scripture aloud during the service.
case_b.consequence_summary: The reading is delivered slowly and clearly for the congregation.
case_b.rule_summary: Offer a public service task faithfully.

gold.adjudication_note: The public service act is the same, but case B is driven by spiritual self-display rather than service.

metadata.author: codex_pilot_probe_v0
metadata.held_constant: The public service act and immediate congregational effect are held constant across A and B.
metadata.changed_dimension: Only the inward motive changes across the pair.

case_a.text: Omar apologizes to his sister after snapping at her because he regrets speaking harshly and wants to repair the relationship.
case_a.outward_act_summary: Apologizes to his sister after snapping at her.
case_a.motive_summary: Repair the relationship and acknowledge real fault.
case_a.consequence_summary: His sister receives an apology from him.
case_a.rule_summary: Apologize after wronging a family member.

case_b.text: Omar apologizes to his sister after snapping at her because he wants their parents to stop criticizing him, not because he thinks he was really wrong.
case_b.outward_act_summary: Apologizes to his sister after snapping at her.
case_b.motive_summary: Shut down criticism without real remorse.
case_b.consequence_summary: His sister receives an apology from him.
case_b.rule_summary: Apologize after wronging a family member.

gold.adjudication_note: Both cases comply with the same outward family norm, but case B treats apology as damage control rather than reconciliation.

metadata.author: codex_pilot_probe_v0
metadata.held_constant: The outward apology and norm compliance are held constant across A and B.
metadata.changed_dimension: Only the heart posture behind the apology changes across the pair.

case_a.text: A coach benches a student for the rest of practice after the student keeps making dangerous tackles because he wants the player and the rest of the team to take safety seriously.
case_a.outward_act_summary: Benches a student for the rest of practice after dangerous behavior.
case_a.consequence_summary: The student is removed from practice for the day.
case_a.rule_summary: Respond firmly to dangerous conduct in practice.

case_b.text: A coach benches a student for the rest of practice after the student keeps making dangerous tackles because he enjoys humiliating the player in front of the team.
case_b.outward_act_summary: Benches a student for the rest of practice after dangerous behavior.
case_b.consequence_summary: The student is removed from practice for the day.
case_b.rule_summary: Respond firmly to dangerous conduct in practice.

gold.adjudication_note: The same disciplinary act has a worse moral meaning when it is aimed at humiliation rather than safety.

metadata.author: codex_pilot_probe_v0
metadata.held_constant: The disciplinary act and immediate practical outcome are held constant across A and B.
metadata.changed_dimension: Only the inward motive changes across the pair.

case_a.text: Rosa drops off groceries for an elderly neighbor because she knows he has trouble getting out and she wants him cared for this week.
case_a.outward_act_summary: Drops off groceries for an elderly neighbor.
case_a.motive_summary: Care for a neighbor who has trouble getting out.
case_a.consequence_summary: The neighbor receives groceries for the week.
case_a.rule_summary: Help a nearby neighbor in practical need.

case_b.text: Rosa drops off groceries for an elderly neighbor because she wants the neighborhood association to notice and praise her for being so generous.
case_b.outward_act_summary: Drops off groceries for an elderly neighbor.
case_b.consequence_summary: The neighbor receives groceries for the week.
case_b.rule_summary: Help a nearby neighbor in practical need.

gold.adjudication_note: The service provided is the same, but case B turns neighbor-care into a bid for recognition.

metadata.author: codex_pilot_probe_v0
metadata.held_constant: The grocery delivery and immediate benefit to the neighbor are held constant across A and B.
metadata.changed_dimension: Only the inward motive changes across the pair.

case_a.text: Ben messages a friend privately after seeing a worrying late-night post because he wants to check whether the friend is okay.
case_a.outward_act_summary: Messages a friend privately after a worrying post.
case_a.motive_summary: Check on a friend who may be struggling.
case_a.consequence_summary: The friend receives a private check-in message.
case_a.rule_summary: Reach out to a friend who may need support.

case_b.text: Ben messages a friend privately after seeing a worrying late-night post because he wants screenshots proving he was one of the first people to step in.
case_b.outward_act_summary: Messages a friend privately after a worrying post.
case_b.motive_summary: Collect proof of his own visible concern.
case_b.consequence_summary: The friend receives a private check-in message.
case_b.rule_summary: Reach out to a friend who may need support.

gold.adjudication_note: The check-in message is the same, but case B treats another persons distress as an opportunity for self-credit.

metadata.author: codex_pilot_probe_v0
metadata.held_constant: The private outreach message and immediate contact are held constant across A and B.
metadata.changed_dimension: Only the inward motive changes across the pair.
